About

I am a mechanical engineer by training with a strong foundation in quantitative methods, machine learning, and data-driven decision making. My MSc thesis at King's College London earned a distinction and focused on AI-powered investment decision support using machine learning with an emphasis on model explainability.

I co-authored peer-reviewed research on thermodynamic modelling of high-temperature heat pump systems, published in Springer conference proceedings. I have also applied engineering thinking in industry through my work at Kirfu, an AI logistics startup.

I volunteer with Engineers Without Borders and the Haya Community Centre, applying technical skills to community development.

Thesis: AI-powered investment decision support system using machine learning, with a focus on model explainability (XAI) to make predictions interpretable for end users.

BEng Mechanical Engineering
City St George's, University of London
Sep 2020 – Jun 2024 · Upper Second Class (2.1)
See details

Four-year accredited BEng programme covering thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, structural analysis, control systems, and materials science.

Developed strong quantitative and computational skills through modules in engineering mathematics, CAD modelling, and finite element analysis.

Final year project applied thermodynamic principles to heat pump system design, forming the basis of subsequent published research.
